name: The Final Tranquility of the Storm

system.description:
Discipline: Qinggong
Skill: Ritual (any) TN 4
Type: Normal
Qi: 6

In order to use this Technique, it must be raining. You position falling raindrops in a specific pattern known only to those with this Technique. To others, you look a fool who runs about (admittedly quickly) redirecting raindrops all over the place. Using a Move and Skill action, you roll any Ritual Skill and choose one target who is currently wet from the storm you are in and has no significant barrier between him and the sky (a roof would count, an umbrella would not). Regardless of your roll, the pattern you created begins to glow a brilliant golden color. The Technique’s effect becomes apparent on your next turn (you may still take actions, the Ritual just took time to work). If you succeeded, the target you chose is struck by lightning for 6d10 Open Damage. If you failed, you are struck by lightning for 6d10 Open Damage. If the Attack deals Damage to anyone, the glow disappears. If it does not (see DREAMING HEAV- ENLY SPLENDOR, a Technique which counters this) the glowing design remains for one hour. Cathartic: You may add one additional target per Rank of Qinggong. Roll independently for each target, and all Failures strike you.
system.source: Wandering Heroes of Ogre Gate, Chapter 3: Kung Fu Techniques
system.accessNotes: You position falling raindrops in a specific pattern known only to those with this Technique.
